audit_items.proof_trace: All free() calls in acl.c are safe: ACLResetSubcommands nulls u->allowed_subcommands after zfree (L563), ACLResetSubcommandsForCommand nulls the per-command slot after zfree (L546), and ACLFreeUserAndKillClients reassigns c->user to DefaultUser before ACLFreeUser(u) (L240-245). The only theoretical window—Users["default"] referencing freed memory between ACLFreeUser(new) at L1268 and raxInsert at L1269 in ACLLoadFromFile—is unexploitable because Redis processes commands single-threadedly and no signal handler or callback accesses the Users rax between those two consecutive lines. Memory leaks exist in ACLSetUser for duplicate >password (L701-706) and ~pattern (L722-726) cases, but these are CWE-401 leaks, not use-after-free violations.
